purpose: Static rules that redden Verification-agreement breaches in verification support and in the seams verification drives. Green verification debt reaches the Quartermaster only as failing verification: a new debt kind lands here as a rule entry, never as a new scenario. The Captain extends this file when routing a verification-debt finding.

rules.clause: Verification agreement, Reuse: contain real creation of an expensive external resource to the one seam that creates it, and reuse it.
rules.reddens: A build, pack, or package-install invocation reached from a step definition body rather than from the run's one shared provisioner in the test support.

rules.clause: Verification agreement, Reuse: provision ambient state once and share it behind a lock or marker file.
rules.reddens: A step definition provisions ambient state that no scenario asserts, where a once-per-run shared provisioner already exists for it.
rules.exempt: A scenario whose behaviour under assertion is the provisioning itself.

rules.clause: Verification agreement, Signals: give real-service steps explicit budgets sized to real latency.
rules.reddens: A step definition that awaits a live crew-session turn and declares no explicit timeout, or declares one shorter than the live-step budget.

rules.clause: Verification agreement, Signals: end every wait on an observed signal; where none is observable, retry in short bounded intervals toward a deadline.
rules.reddens: A literal delay that is neither a bounded retry interval inside a deadline loop nor a drain after an observed settle signal.

rules.clause: Real by default Article; Verification agreement, Proof: a test double stands only under a named condition, marked inline.
rules.reddens: A test double in the verification support carrying no inline mark naming its condition.

rules.clause: Asset policy: product-facing content lives in assets or project-approved content catalogs.
rules.reddens: A prompt argument passed to a role dispatch or a seat session that is a string or template literal rather than a value read from the agent-prompt catalog.
rules.note: Structural, not textual. The methodology-conformance text pattern matches only a literal opening with "You are the/a/an", so a dispatch prompt phrased as an instruction sits in the implementation and passes.
